strengths: Lead is fast and inverted-pyramid: who, what, where, when in the first sentence, properly attributed to Variety.
Core breaking claim is attributed in words ('according to Variety') and not overstated as independently confirmed.
Accurate handling of the source facts — 1,225 episodes, 1966-1971, Barnabas/Frid, Curtis, Burton 2012, Big Finish all check out.
Clean, scannable structure with useful question subheads and tight Key Takeaways.
Honest about the limits of the news ('details were not disclosed') rather than padding with invention.
FAQ adds genuinely new info (DVD/MPI release, Depp as star) not already in the body.
weaknesses: Reads like competent wire-desk filler, not a writer with a pulse — no POV, no wit, no surprise, no single vivid detail brought forward (e.g. the famous 'one big contemporary yawn' Variety pan or the Billboard chart success would have given it life).

Generic AI-tell phrasing: 'signals a deliberate expansion,' 'fertile ground for a new animated interpretation,' 'The show's legacy is significant,' 'Warner Bros. Animation's commitment suggests it views...' — speculative throat-clearing that says nothing.
Flesch 41 confirms denser-than-ideal prose; several sentences stack clauses where a fan wants snap.
Copula-avoidance and nominalizations creep in ('positions the project as being in the earliest stages').
Info gain is thin — the back half is a Wikipedia-grade recap with no original framing or reporter's read on why an adult-animation Dark Shadows is interesting now.
Full name 'Warner Bros. Animation' repeated where the rhythm wanted a pronoun or 'the studio,' adding stiffness.
The 'Where can I watch' FAQ is solid but the closing paragraph guesses at motive rather than reporting it.
